Version.Name: Androgen Receptor-mediated effect (IRFMN-COMPARA)
Version.Summary: Classification model for Androgen Receptor-mediated effect (IRFMN/COMPARA).

Guide.Description: The model provides a qualitative prediction for Androgen Receptor (AR) effects mediated through the AR pathway. The data were used to generate binary classification models to discriminate active (both agonists and antagonists) compounds from inactive ones. The model provides a qualitative prediction for Androgen Receptor (AR) effects mediated through the AR pathway. The data were used to generate binary classification models to discriminate active (both agonists and antagonists) compounds from inactive ones. The model provides a qualitative prediction for Androgen Receptor (AR) effects mediated through the AR pathway. The data were used to generate binary classification models to discriminate active (both agonists and antagonists) compounds from inactive ones.
Guide.Model: The model has been developed by Alessandra Roncaglioni Istituto di ricerche farmacologiche Mario Negri - IRCSS on 19 December 2018.
Guide.Dataset: Model is built based on in vitro high-throughput screening (HTS) assays measuring activity of chemicals at multiple points along the androgen receptor (AR) activity pathway. Chemical data were collected from the open free library ToxCast/Tox21 (https://www.epa.gov/chemical-research/toxcast-dashboard).
Guide.Descriptors:
Structural fragment features, it is a two steps model developed with SARpy. In the first step SARpy was used to model the two classes, identifying a set of 127 rules (17 for active and 110 for inactive). Then, a second set of 22 rules identifying active compounds is applied to unpredicted compounds only. Compounds are labelled as inactive if matched ether by an inactive SA in the first step or not matched by the latter step. More precisely: If a compound matches active class when applying the first ruleset the output is Active; If a compound matches inactive class when applying the first ruleset the output is Inctive; If a compound matches active class when applying the second ruleset the output is Probably Active; If a compound does not match any class when applying the first ruleset nor the second one the output is Probably Inactive. The first set of structural alerts was extracted relative only for the active class. The second set of structural alerts was extracted taking in account both class. Fragment are generated using SARPy software. [SARpy software SARpy software (for secondary and tertiary fragment) https://www.vegahub.eu/portfolio-item/sarpy/ 4.Defining the algorithm - OECD Principle 2].
1667 chemicals/ 149 structural alerts

AD.Name: Similar_Molecules
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: strongly similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: only moderately similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: no similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found

AD.Name: Accuracy
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is good
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not optimal
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not adequate

AD.Name: Concordance
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that agree with the target compound predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that slightly disagree with the target compound predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that completely disagree with the target compound predicted value

AD.Name: ACF_SimCheck
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: all atom centered fragment of the compound have been found in the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some atom centered fragment of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: a prominent number of atom centered fragments of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments

AD.Name: AD_Index
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: predicted substance is into the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: predicted substance could be out of the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: predicted substance is out of the the Applicability Domain of the model

xml.Reference: It has been described here: Development, validation and integration of in silico models to identify androgen active chemicals https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0045653518324664?via%3Dihub For a general description of the VEGA software see: Benfenati E, Manganaro A, Gini G VEGA-QSAR: AI inside a platform for predictive toxicology Proceedings of the workshop "Popularize Artificial Intelligence 2013", December 5th 2013, Turin, Italy Published on CEUR Workshop Proceedings Vol-1107
xml.Endpoint: Data are from Human. The model provides a qualitative prediction of effect of compound on Androgen receptor.
